one_line_takeaway: RENNERVATE detects and sanitizes indirect prompt injection at the token level by training a lightweight (0.5-0.8M parameter) classifier on the target LLM's own multi-head attention patterns, beating 15 classifier-, LLM-, and prompt/model-modification-based baselines across 5 LLMs while requiring no LLM fine-tuning, though it needs white-box attention access that closed-API users must approximate via a local shadow LLM.
key_finding: On the FIPI test set, RENNERVATE achieves detection accuracies of 99.05% (ChatGLM), 97.88% (Dolly), 99.58% (Falcon), 99.43% (LLaMA2), 99.37% (LLaMA3), with FPR 0.46%-2.42% and FNR 0.30%-1.82% across all 5 models; on LLaMA3 it beats the best classifier baselines Attention Tracker (83.23%) and TaskTracker (95.07%) by 16.14 and 4.30 accuracy points respectively, and beats the best LLM-based detectors GPT-Resp (82.55%) and DS-Resp (91.71%). For sanitization, the 5 IPI attack types achieve total (undefended) ASRs of 85.90% (ChatGLM), 72.10% (Dolly), 84.90% (Falcon), 67.10% (LLaMA2), 60.80% (LLaMA3); RENNERVATE reduces these ASRs by 85.80/72.10/84.90/66.90/60.60 percentage points respectively, outperforming the best prompt-modification baseline (Spotlighting, which only lowers Falcon's ASR from 84.90% to 35.10%) and the best LLM-based sanitizer GPT-Loc (ASR reductions of 75.60/63.90/75.10/57.40/51.50 pts), and performing comparably to the model-modification baseline StruQ (66.80% ASR reduction on LLaMA2, vs. RENNERVATE's 66.90% without any LLM fine-tuning). Sanitized text retains high fidelity to benign text (Jaccard similarity mostly 0.9-1.0) and near-neutral AlpacaEval2.0 win rates versus benign outputs (43.60%-46.78%, i.e., close to the 50% parity baseline). Against unseen attacks (GCG, Neural Exec) across FIPI and 4 unseen datasets, RENNERVATE's sanitization drops undefended ASRs of 83.00-100.0% down to 0.00-7.00%. Against adaptive black-box attacks, undefended PAIR/TAP ASRs of 94.00-100.0% across all 5 LLMs are reduced to 0.00-19.00% (0% on ChatGLM and LLaMA2 for both attacks). Against the white-box adaptive GCG variant with a detection-evasion loss specifically targeting RENNERVATE, undefended ASRs of 93.00-99.50% across 5 datasets drop to 0.00-5.00% with RENNERVATE deployed. Ablations show the 2-step attentive pooling and mean filter are both necessary components, m as small as a few response tokens is sufficient (accuracy improves further with more), and while more residual blocks (N up to 16) improve in-distribution FIPI accuracy, this comes at a transferability cost (e.g., 9.95-point accuracy drop on Jfleg-RTE when N increases from 2 to 16). Reported parameter size of the detector is 0.5-0.8M, described as compact relative to baselines (Table XIII in the paper, not itemized here).
core_contribution: RENNERVATE, a defense framework against Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) attacks on LLM-integrated applications, that detects and sanitizes injected instructions at the token level using the target LLM's own internal attention features rather than an auxiliary classifier LLM or prompt rewriting. It has three components: (1) a Token-Level Detector Cθ that classifies each input token fi as injected/clean using a '2-step attentive pooling' mechanism (Resp-Wise Attentive Pooling over the first m=32 response tokens' attention, followed by Head-Wise Attentive Pooling over attention heads/layers), feeding N=2 residual blocks (hidden dim 512) trained with cross-entropy loss; (2) an Injection Identifier that mean-filters (kernel k=5) the per-token logits and flags X as 'Injected' if the longest run of injected tokens exceeds a Threshold=5; (3) an Injection Sanitizer that removes the flagged tokens F* from F and detokenizes the remainder to produce sanitized text X. The authors also construct and plan to open-source FIPI, a fine-grained token-level-labeled IPI dataset (100,000 injected + 10,000 benign instances) built by extending the SEP dataset with a 5-step 'probe-witness' construction pipeline, covering 5 attack types (Naive, Escape Characters, Context Ignoring, Fake Completion, and 3 combined variants) across 300 NLP subtasks.
threat_model: Adversary: full control over external data sources retrieved by the LLM-integrated agent; goal is to manipulate the agent's backend LLM response via injected instructions in that data (IPI, not direct prompt injection into the user's own instruction). Knowledge/capability levels evaluated range from black-box prompt-engineering attacks (Naive, Escape Characters, Context Ignoring, Fake Completion, and combinations) up to adaptive adversaries: black-box PAIR and TAP (LLM-driven iterative jailbreak/injection search, up to 20 and 70 queries respectively) and a white-box GCG variant augmented with a custom detection-evasion loss term (500 optimization iterations) that has gradient access to RENNERVATE's own token-level detector. Defender: can observe the target LLM's attention weights (white-box access to the LLM, i.e., token embeddings and per-layer/per-head attention scores) but cannot modify the target LLM itself, and has no knowledge of the specific attack method, wording, or injection position used by the adversary.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations (Section VII): (1) RENNERVATE requires white-box access to internal attention weights of the target LLM, which is not available to end users of closed proprietary APIs; for such users the paper proposes either relying on a provider-hosted implementation (not verified in this paper) or running a separate 'shadow LLM' (e.g., ChatGLM-6B) locally as a proxy, adding deployment complexity and a potential mismatch between the shadow model's attention patterns and the actual API model's; (2) sanitization does not always fully remove injected-task-specific content, which can degrade utility on certain tasks such as grammar correction; (3) the paper explicitly scopes itself to text-only IPI and leaves multi-modal IPI attacks (images, audio) as future work, since attention-based detection has not been shown to generalize there; (4) it leaves 'recovery' from more severe compromise as future work, describing its token-removal sanitization as only 'an initial attempt.' Independently noted from the results themselves: the white-box adaptive GCG evaluation is conducted only on LLaMA2 (single model), so robustness against gradient-based adversaries with detection-loss access is not shown across all 5 architectures; StruQ, the strongest model-modification competitor, could likewise only be evaluated on LLaMA2 due to limited official model availability, limiting the breadth of that comparison.

datasets_used: FIPI (paper's own dataset: 100,000 injected + 10,000 benign instances, extended from SEP with probe-witness token-level labels, 5 attack types + 3 combined variants, 300 NLP subtasks)
SEP (base dataset extended to build FIPI: 9,160 user-instruction/clean-data pairs, 3 task categories x 100 subtasks)
MRPC (unseen transferability dataset, duplicate sentence detection)
HSOL (unseen transferability dataset, hate content detection)
RTE (unseen transferability dataset, natural language inference)
quantitative_results: FIPI detection accuracy: 99.05% ChatGLM, 97.88% Dolly, 99.58% Falcon, 99.43% LLaMA2, 99.37% LLaMA3
FIPI detection FPR 0.46%-2.42%, FNR 0.30%-1.82% across all 5 LLMs
Undefended total ASR: 85.90% ChatGLM, 72.10% Dolly, 84.90% Falcon, 67.10% LLaMA2, 60.80% LLaMA3
RENNERVATE ASR reduction: 85.80 ChatGLM, 72.10 Dolly, 84.90 Falcon, 66.90 LLaMA2, 60.60 LLaMA3 (percentage points)
AlpacaEval2.0 win rate of sanitized vs benign outputs: 46.37%, 44.34%, 44.59%, 43.60%, 46.78% (near 50% parity)
Jaccard similarity of sanitized vs benign text: predominantly 0.9-1.0
Unseen attacks (GCG/Neural Exec) undefended ASR 83.00-100.0% reduced to 0.00-7.00% with RENNERVATE sanitization across FIPI+4 unseen datasets
Black-box adaptive attacks (PAIR/TAP): undefended ASR 94.00-100.0% reduced to 0.00-19.00% (0% on ChatGLM and LLaMA2)
White-box adaptive GCG (with detection-evasion loss): undefended ASR 93.00-99.50% across 5 datasets reduced to 0.00-5.00% with RENNERVATE
Ablation: residual blocks N=2->16 improves FIPI accuracy but drops transferability accuracy by 9.95 pts (Jfleg-RTE) and 7.00 pts (RTE-Jfleg)
Detector parameter size: 0.5-0.8M (compact relative to baselines per paper's Table XIII)
baselines_compared: Attention Tracker (classifier-based detection, 83.23% acc on LLaMA3)
GPT-Resp / DS-Resp (Response-Based Detection, 82.55%/91.71% acc on LLaMA3)
Spotlighting/datamarking (prompt-modification, best ASR reduction on Falcon: 84.90%->35.10%)
StruQ (model-modification, evaluated only on LLaMA2, 66.80% ASR reduction)
PAIR (black-box adaptive attack, not a defense baseline)
TAP (black-box adaptive attack, not a defense baseline)
GCG with detection-evasion loss (white-box adaptive attack, not a defense baseline)
